channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump’s billionaire pick to lead NASA sparks debate over the future of space exploration
item.description: Jared Isaacman, billionaire, CEO and nominee to become the next NASA administrator, faced questions on April 9, 2025, from members of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation during his confirmation hearing for the position.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: For April, a large ‘dynamic’ Patch Tuesday release
item.description: This month’s Patch Tuesday release is large (126 patches), broad and unfortunately very dynamic, with several re-releases, missing files and broken patches affecting both the Windows and Office platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Musk 'most unlikeable person' in US: House Democrat
item.description: Rep. Robert Garcia (D-Calif.) called Elon Musk the “most unlikeable person” in the U.S. during a Thursday speech in support of his party’s push to oust the tech giant from his White House role by May 30.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: OnePlus Watch 3 gets surprise price hike in the US – and you can already guess why
item.description: The OnePlus Watch 3 now costs $499.99 in the US following the introduction of major tariffs on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft Gets Approval for North San Jose Data Center Project
item.description: Microsoft has received approval to move forward with its North San Jose data center project, marking the first time the tech giant will build, own and manage a digital infrastructure facility in the South Bay.
item.dc:creator: Devan Patel, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple airlifted 600 tons of iPhones from India to the U.S. Here’s why
item.description: Tech giant Apple chartered cargo flights to ferry 600 tons of iPhones, or as many as 1.5 million, to the United States from India, after it stepped up production there in an effort to beat President Donald Trump’s tariffs, sources told Reuters. The details of the push provide an insight into the U.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects
item.description: Microsoft said on Wednesday it is "slowing or pausing" some of its data center projects, describing the move as a show of flexibility as the artificial intelligence (AI) industry evolves.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again
item.description: If DOGE is looking for a high-value, high-return target, the software procurement process is an easy win. The post A quick win for DOGE? Make procurement competitive again first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: Dozens of House Democrats are demanding President Trump confirm that Elon Musk will leave his role in the administration by May 30. A group of 77 lawmakers, led by Rep.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: TBM helps CIOs translate tech spending to business outcomes
item.description: IT environments are constantly growing more complex, with organizations rolling out AI agents and LLMs, operating in multiple clouds, and anticipating breakthroughs like quantum computing.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Trump's trade war with China will hit tech prices
item.description: President Trump’s escalating trade war with China is putting a spotlight on the various technology companies and products poised to feel the ripple effects of the ongoing conflict.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Adding smarts to Azure data lakes with Fabric Data Agents
item.description: Enterprise AI needs one thing if it’s to get around the limitations of large language models and deliver the results businesses need from their agents.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies
item.description: Gina Scinta, the deputy CTO at Thales Trusted Cyber Technologies, says new survey data provides insights for how agencies better secure data and networks. The post New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Hydrogen fuel cell trucks hit the roads in Georgia
item.description: Hydrogen trucks debut in Georgia, sparking debates on costs and green freight gains. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson comments on this promising chapter in green logistics evolution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11
